classData.comment:
/**
 * @summary Configuration manager for the GitHub Workflow MCP server.
 *
 * Supports loading configuration from a custom file and merging with defaults.
 * The configuration handles GitHub repository details, sync settings, and server behavior options.
 *
 * @class Neo.ai.mcp.server.github-workflow.Config
 * @extends Neo.ai.ConfigProvider
 * @singleton
 */
classData.summary:
Configuration manager for the GitHub Workflow MCP server.

Supports loading configuration from a custom file and merging with defaults.
The configuration handles GitHub repository details, sync settings, and server behavior options.
classData.name: Config

classData.comment:
/**
         * @member {String} className='Neo.ai.mcp.server.github-workflow.Config'
         * @protected
         */
classData.name: className

classData.comment:
/**
         * @member {Boolean} singleton=true
         * @protected
         */
classData.name: singleton
data

classData.description: The root directory of the project.
classData.name: projectRoot

classData.description: Global debug flag for all MCP servers.
classData.name: debug

classData.description: Minimum stderr log level for the GitHub workflow logger.
classData.name: logLevel

classData.comment:
/**
             * @summary Shared MCP logger policy for GitHub Workflow.
             *
             * Priority-filtered stderr only. `debug: true` promotes the default `warn`
             * threshold to `debug`; no file sink is used for this workflow server.
             * @type {Object}
             */
classData.summary:
Shared MCP logger policy for GitHub Workflow.

Priority-filtered stderr only. `debug: true` promotes the default `warn`
threshold to `debug`; no file sink is used for this workflow server.
classData.name: logger

classData.description: Cache duration for healthy health checks (in milliseconds). Unhealthy results are never cached.
classData.name: healthCheckCacheDuration

classData.description: The minimum required version of the GitHub CLI (gh).
classData.name: minGhVersion

classData.description: The owner of the GitHub repository.
classData.name: owner

classData.description: The name of the GitHub repository.
classData.name: repo

classData.description: Whether to automatically trigger a full sync when the server starts.
classData.name: syncOnStartup

classData.description: Whether to automatically commit and push changes after a sync. Only executes if the user has write permissions and there are non-metadata changes.
classData.name: pushToRepoAfterSync

classData.description: Configuration for the issue synchronization service.
classData.name: issueSync

classData.description: The root directory for synced content.
classData.name: contentRoot

classData.description: The path to the directory for active issues.
classData.name: issuesDir

classData.description: The root directory for version-based archives across all entities.
classData.name: archiveRoot

classData.description: The path to the directory for discussions.
classData.name: discussionsDir

classData.description: The path to the directory for pull requests.
classData.name: pullsDir

classData.description: The path to the synchronization metadata file.
classData.name: metadataFile

classData.description: Labels that, when present on an issue, will cause it to be ignored and deleted locally.
classData.name: droppedLabels

classData.comment:
/**
                 * Containment denylist: discussions whose `number` or `author.login` match are excluded
                 * from sync — never written to `resources/content/**` or downstream KB chunks. Cached
                 * copies are quarantined (file + content-index entry removed) by `number` even when
                 * GitHub no longer lists them; `author` matching is fetch-time exclusion only (the sync
                 * cache persists `number`, not author). Policy-free; the empty default is a no-op.
                 * @type {{numbers: Number[], authors: String[]}}
                 */
classData.description: Containment denylist: discussions whose number or author.login match are excluded from sync — never written to resources/content/** or downstream KB chunks. Cached copies are quarantined (file + content-index entry removed) by number even when GitHub no longer lists them; author matching is fetch-time exclusion only (the sync cache persists number, not author). Policy-free; the empty default is a no-op.
classData.name: discussionDenylist

classData.description: Product names to redact from untrusted GitHub-authored content when the content-trust sanitizer projects sync/write-boundary Markdown. Empty by default; policy values belong in local config, not in syncer code.
classData.name: productNameDenylist

classData.description: The date from which to start synchronizing issues and releases.
classData.name: syncStartDate

classData.description: The path to the directory for release notes.
classData.name: releaseNotesDir

classData.description: A prefix for issue filenames to prevent them from starting with a number (e.g., 'issue-').
classData.name: issueFilenamePrefix
discussionFilenamePrefix

classData.description: A prefix for version-based archive directories (e.g., 'v' for 'v1.2.3'). Applies to both milestone titles and release tags.
classData.name: versionDirectoryPrefix

classData.description: Whether closed issues and pull requests may use semver milestone titles as an archive-bucket fallback when release-date bucketing finds no cut release.
classData.name: routeByMilestone

classData.description: The maximum number of items per chunk directory in the archive.
classData.name: archiveChunkThreshold

classData.description: A prefix for archive chunk directories (e.g., 'chunk-').
classData.name: archiveChunkPrefix

classData.description: A prefix for release note filenames (e.g., 'v').
classData.name: releaseFilenamePrefix

classData.comment:
/**
                 * The maximum number of issues to fetch from the GitHub API in a single sync.
                 * Defensive ceiling against runaway pagination on a misconfigured GraphQL pageInfo while
                 * leaving enough headroom for clean-slate exhaustive emission under the archive contract.
                 * The local droppedLabels filter further trims the actual processed set.
                 * @type {number}
                 */
classData.description: The maximum number of issues to fetch from the GitHub API in a single sync. Defensive ceiling against runaway pagination on a misconfigured GraphQL pageInfo while leaving enough headroom for clean-slate exhaustive emission under the archive contract. The local droppedLabels filter further trims the actual processed set.
classData.name: maxIssues

classData.comment:
/**
                 * Safety cap on releases fetched from the GitHub API. Must exceed the repo's total
                 * release count: the closed-item bucketing reference (`ReleaseNotesSyncer.sortedReleases`)
                 * spans the full history, so a cap below the total would drop the oldest releases and
                 * mis-bucket pre-cap closed items.
                 * @type {number}
                 */
classData.description: Safety cap on releases fetched from the GitHub API. Must exceed the repo's total release count: the closed-item bucketing reference (ReleaseNotesSyncer.sortedReleases) spans the full history, so a cap below the total would drop the oldest releases and mis-bucket pre-cap closed items.
classData.name: maxReleases

classData.description: The number of releases to fetch per page in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: releaseQueryLimit

classData.description: The maximum buffer size for the gh CLI command output.
classData.name: maxGhOutputBuffer

classData.description: The markdown delimiter used to separate the issue body from the comments section.
classData.name: commentSectionDelimiter

classData.description: Maximum number of labels to fetch per issue in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: maxLabelsPerIssue

classData.description: Maximum number of labels to fetch for the entire repository in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: maxRepoLabels

classData.description: Maximum number of assignees to fetch per issue in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: maxAssigneesPerIssue

classData.description: Maximum number of sub-issues to fetch per issue in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: maxSubIssuesPerIssue

classData.description: Maximum number of timeline items to fetch per issue in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: maxTimelineItemsPerIssue

classData.description: Configuration for pull request queries.
classData.name: pullRequest

classData.description: Default values for pull request queries.
classData.name: defaults

classData.description: The default number of pull requests to return.
classData.name: limit

classData.description: The default state of pull requests to list.
classData.name: state

classData.description: The maximum number of pull requests that can be fetched in a single API call.
classData.name: maxLimit

classData.description: Maximum number of comments to fetch per pull request in GraphQL queries.
classData.name: maxCommentsPerPullRequest
